failure_modes.text: Complexity can become an alibi when the team talks about the system without naming concrete leverage points, experiments, or ownership.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger When “The System Is Complex” Becomes an Alibi.
failure_modes.mitigation: Require every complexity framing to name concrete leverage points, experiments, or owners; complexity without action items is an alibi.

failure_modes.text: Mechanical or bureaucratic thinking can misdesign social systems by treating them like static machines with fixed parts.
failure_modes.source_quote: The Bureaucratic Mindset: Applying mechanical or linear beliefs to social systems is counterproductive.
failure_modes.mitigation: Design social systems with feedback loops, adaptation mechanisms, and agency in mind rather than treating them as static machines with fixed parts.

failure_modes.text: Adaptive-system analysis can drown in overload when too many variables are tracked without deciding which loop or boundary matters most.
failure_modes.mitigation: Limit the scope of adaptive-system analysis to the loops and boundaries that drive the decision; tracking everything drowns the signal.

premortem_questions.text: What would have to be true for this system view to explain the behavior we are seeing?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What would you have to believe?

premortem_questions.text: Which feedback loop, delay, or incentive are we leaving out that could change how this intervention behaves after we act?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Works when feedback, delays, and incentives keep reshaping the environment.

premortem_questions.text: Are we telling the story of the search, or can we state the core system loop clearly enough for someone else to act on it?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Am I telling the story of the search (the complex, winding analysis) or the story of the solution (the clear, simple outcome)?

heuristics.text: Use a five-step adaptive loop that separates goals, problems, diagnosis, design, and doing so the system can learn instead of thrash.
Map the system visually so interactions, boundaries, and causal loops become explicit instead of remaining implicit assumptions.
Expose the assumptions behind the current system view before acting so the team knows which beliefs are carrying the intervention.
heuristics.source_quote: A key heuristic is to use the “What would you have to believe?” question to spell out all the implicit assumptions in a perspective.

curation_notes.summary: Focused on adaptive loops, system maps, and anti-complexity-theater discipline rather than on simulation or digital-twin sprawl.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor simulation overlays because the raw source is stronger on adaptive loops, assumption exposure, and systems mapping.
open_questions: The second premortem question compacts several adjacent feedback, delay, and incentive passages into one reusable check.

allies: The raw allies section is strong on systems-thinking, leverage-points, and iterative adaptation for later relation preservation.
antagonists: The raw antagonists section is strongest on confirmation-bias and shortsighted single-step reasoning, but only the cleanest current-vocabulary ids should survive Wave 3.
structured_tensions: The source contains an explicit structured tension block that includes optionality and confirmation-bias.
